title: Heating Circuit / Control Board Error
shortDescription: The control board has detected a fault in the heating circuit.
fullDescription: The E01 error code indicates a fault in the dishwasher's heating system or control board. The control board is unable to properly control the heating element. This could be a relay failure on the board, a wiring issue, or a failed heating element.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then turn it back on and run a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element under the tub. Test for continuity with a multimeter (should read 10-30 ohms). Also check for continuity to ground (should be none).

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all wiring connections between the control board and the heating element for loose, burned, or corroded terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the heating element if it fails the continuity test, or the control board if the element tests good.

whenToCallPro: If the control board needs replacement or if the heating element is inaccessible without significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E01 heating error fix

faq.q: Is E01 the same on Siemens and Bosch dishwashers?
faq.a: Yes, Siemens and Bosch share the same BSH platform and use identical error codes.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher with E01?
faq.a: The dishwasher may run but water will not be heated, resulting in poor cleaning and sanitization.

faq.q: How do I access the heating element?
faq.a: The heating element is located under the tub. Access it by removing the lower front panel and reaching underneath.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water temperature sensor (NTC) has failed.
fullDescription: The E02 error code indicates the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) temperature sensor is providing readings outside the expected range. The sensor may be open circuit, short circuit, or reading inaccurately. Without proper temperature feedback, the dishwasher cannot regulate heating.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, turn it back on, and test a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Locate the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is usually integrated into the heating element assembly or located in the sump area. Disconnect power and access the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the NTC wires and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (20-25C), it should read approximately 5,000-6,000 ohms.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the resistance is significantly different from expected, replace the sensor. Some models have it integrated with the heater and both must be replaced together.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor replacement does not clear the error.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E02 NTC sensor fix

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: An NTC sensor changes resistance based on temperature. It allows the control board to know the water temperature.

faq.q: Can I test the sensor without removing it?
faq.a: You can test it by disconnecting the wires at the connector and measuring resistance at the connector.

faq.q: Where is the NTC sensor located?
faq.a: It is typically in the sump area at the bottom of the dishwasher, often integrated with or near the heating element.

title: Fill Timeout / Water Supply Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not fill with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: The E03 error code indicates the dishwasher was unable to fill with enough water within the timeout period. Water may not be entering at all or entering too slowly.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply valve under the sink is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the inlet hose for kinks. If using an Aquastop hose, check that the Aquastop has not been triggered.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the water supply, remove the inlet hose from the dishwasher, and clean the mesh filter screen.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the inlet valve solenoid. Replace if it does not have continuity.

whenToCallPro: If the Aquastop hose has been triggered and needs replacement, or the inlet valve is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E03 water fill error fix

faq.q: Why is my Siemens dishwasher not filling?
faq.a: Check the water supply valve, inlet hose, and filter screen first. These are the most common causes.

faq.q: What is an Aquastop hose?
faq.a: An Aquastop hose has a built-in electric valve that only opens when the dishwasher needs water. If it detects a leak, it shuts off permanently.

faq.q: Can a triggered Aquastop be reset?
faq.a: Most Aquastop hoses cannot be reset once triggered and need to be replaced.

title: Flow Sensor / Turbidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow or turbidity sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E04 error code indicates the flow sensor or turbidity sensor (water clarity sensor) is not providing expected readings. This sensor monitors water flow through the spray arms and/or how dirty the water is to optimize the wash cycle.
symptoms: Auto programs do not adjust properly

fixSteps.title: Clean spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove both upper and lower spray arms. Clean all nozzle holes with a toothpick. Rinse under running water.

fixSteps.title: Clean the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the turbidity sensor in the sump area (a small glass or plastic window). Clean it with a soft cloth and warm water.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Check the wiring connections to the flow/turbidity sensor are secure.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, the sensor may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor needs replacement and is difficult to access.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E04 flow sensor fix

faq.q: What does the turbidity sensor do?
faq.a: It measures how dirty the water is, allowing auto programs to adjust cycle time and water usage for optimal cleaning.

faq.q: Can clogged spray arms trigger E04?
faq.a: Yes, if spray arms cannot produce adequate flow, the flow sensor may detect insufficient water circulation.

faq.q: How often should I clean spray arms?
faq.a: Clean spray arms every 2-3 months or whenever you notice reduced cleaning performance.

title: Door Switch / Aquastop Error
shortDescription: The door switch or Aquastop system has detected an issue.
fullDescription: The E06 error code indicates either the door switch is not detecting a closed door, or the Aquastop system has detected a potential leak. The dishwasher requires a confirmed closed and latched door before operation.
symptoms: Door appears closed but error persists

fixSteps.title: Close door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door and close it firmly, pushing until you hear the latch click into place.

fixSteps.title: Check door latch alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door latch mechanism and the strike plate on the tub for proper alignment. Tighten any loose screws.

fixSteps.title: Check Aquastop hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the Aquastop inlet hose. If the indicator light on the Aquastop is red, the hose has been triggered and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Test the door switch with a multimeter for continuity when the door is closed. Replace if faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the Aquastop has triggered and needs hose replacement, or if the latch mechanism is broken.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E06 door Aquastop error fix

faq.q: How do I know if Aquastop has triggered?
faq.a: Check the Aquastop hose at the water supply connection. A red indicator means it has been triggered.

faq.q: Can I reset the Aquastop?
faq.a: Most Siemens Aquastop hoses are single-use and cannot be reset. The entire hose must be replaced.

faq.q: Why does the door not latch properly?
faq.a: The latch may be worn, misaligned, or the strike plate on the tub may have shifted. Check both components.

title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The heating element has failed or has a fault.
fullDescription: The E09 error code specifically indicates the heating element has failed. The control board has tested the heater circuit and determined the heating element has an open circuit, short circuit, or ground fault. The element must be replaced for the dishwasher to heat water and dry dishes.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, turn back on, and test.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element from underneath. Test for continuity (10-30 ohms) and test for ground fault (no continuity to frame).

fixSteps.title: Check element connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring terminals on the heating element for corrosion, burns, or looseness.

fixSteps.title: Replace heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the retaining nut and push the element into the tub. Remove from inside and install the new element. Ensure the gasket seats properly.

whenToCallPro: If you are not comfortable accessing the underside of the dishwasher or if the control board is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E09 heating element replacement

faq.q: Is E09 the same as E01?
faq.a: E09 specifically indicates a heating element failure, while E01 is a more general heating circuit error that could also involve the control board.

faq.q: Can limescale damage the heating element?
faq.a: Yes, heavy limescale buildup can cause hotspots on the element, leading to premature burnout. Regular descaling helps.

faq.q: Will my dishes still get clean without heating?
faq.a: Cold water washing is much less effective. Dishes will not be properly sanitized and grease may not be fully removed.

title: Drying Fan / Ventilation Error
shortDescription: The drying system has a fault.
fullDescription: The E10 error code indicates a problem with the drying system. On models with active drying (fan or condenser), this means the drying component is not operating correctly. The dishwasher may still wash but will not dry dishes effectively.
symptoms: No fan sound during dry phase

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check ventilation
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the dishwasher vent (usually on the inner door) for blockages. Clean any debris from the vent area.

fixSteps.title: Test drying fan
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Locate the drying fan and check the wiring connections. Test the fan motor with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace drying fan
fixSteps.instruction: If the fan motor is faulty, disconnect the wiring and mounting screws and install a new fan assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor needs replacement or the control board is not sending power to the drying system.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E10 drying error fix

faq.q: Do all Siemens dishwashers have a drying fan?
faq.a: No, only some models have active drying with a fan. Basic models use passive condensation drying.

faq.q: Can I still use the dishwasher with E10?
faq.a: Yes, dishes will still be washed but may not dry completely. You may need to towel-dry them.

faq.q: How does condensation drying work?
faq.a: The stainless steel tub absorbs heat from the final hot rinse, causing moisture on dishes to condense on the cooler tub walls and drain away.

title: Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water flow sensor is not detecting proper water flow.
fullDescription: The E14 error code indicates the flow sensor is not detecting water flow when the fill valve is open. This could mean the fill valve is not opening, the flow sensor has failed, or there is a blockage preventing water from reaching the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply valve is open and the inlet hose is not kinked.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the mesh filter at the water connection point.

fixSteps.title: Test the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and access the flow sensor. Check the wiring and test the sensor with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If faulty, disconnect the sensor wiring and water connections, remove the old sensor, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If both the sensor and inlet valve need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E14 flow sensor error fix

faq.q: Where is the flow sensor?
faq.a: The flow sensor is typically located in the water inlet assembly, near where the supply hose connects to the dishwasher.

faq.q: Can scale block the flow sensor?
faq.a: Yes, mineral deposits can interfere with the sensor. Regular descaling helps prevent this.

faq.q: Is E14 related to E03?
faq.a: Both relate to water fill issues. E14 specifically indicates the flow sensor is not detecting water movement.

title: Water in Base / Leak Detected
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base pan of the dishwasher.
fullDescription: The E15 error code is one of the most common Siemens dishwasher errors. It indicates that the Aquastop float switch in the base of the dishwasher has detected water, meaning there is an internal leak. The dishwasher will continuously run the drain pump and will not operate until the water is removed and the leak is fixed.
symptoms: E15 displayed or tap icon flashing
Dishwasher will not run any cycle

fixSteps.title: Turn off water and power
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and disconnect power to the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Tilt to drain base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully pull the dishwasher forward, tilt it back about 45 degrees to drain water from the base pan. Have towels ready. Hold for 30 seconds then set it level.

fixSteps.title: Find the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the side panels and inspect inside while running a short cycle. Check the door seal, spray arm connections, sump area, and all hose clamps.

fixSteps.title: Fix the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten loose clamps, replace the door seal if worn, or replace any cracked hoses found during inspection.

whenToCallPro: If the leak is from the sump or pump area, which requires significant disassembly.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E15 water in base leak fix

faq.q: Why does my dishwasher pump keep running?
faq.a: With E15, the drain pump runs continuously trying to remove water from the base pan. Turn off power and tilt the unit to drain.

faq.q: Can the Aquastop float get stuck?
faq.a: Yes, the float can stick in the raised position from dried residue. Tilting the dishwasher can free it.

faq.q: Is E15 the most common Siemens error?
faq.a: Yes, E15 is one of the most frequently reported errors. It is often caused by a minor leak that accumulates in the base over time.

title: Uncontrolled Water Fill
shortDescription: The dishwasher is filling with water when it should not be.
fullDescription: The E16 error code indicates the dishwasher has detected water entering when the inlet valve should be closed. This is an uncontrolled fill condition that could lead to overflow. The inlet valve may be stuck open or the control board is not properly controlling the valve.
symptoms: Water fills when machine is off
Dishwasher runs drain pump to compensate

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Close the water supply valve under the sink right away to stop uncontrolled filling.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher off and unplugged but the water supply on, check if water continues to enter the tub. If it does, the valve is stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water. Remove the inlet valve and inspect for debris preventing it from closing. Clean or replace as needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve is stuck open, replace it with a new one. Connect the water supply and wiring to the new valve.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is stuck and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E16 uncontrolled fill fix

faq.q: Is E16 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, uncontrolled water fill can cause flooding. Turn off the water supply immediately when this error appears.

faq.q: Can debris cause the valve to stick open?
faq.a: Yes, small particles from the water supply can prevent the valve from closing fully. A mesh screen filter helps prevent this.

faq.q: Should I keep the water supply off between uses?
faq.a: While not strictly necessary, turning off the water supply when the dishwasher is not in use prevents potential flooding from a stuck valve.

title: Diverter Valve Error
shortDescription: The water diverter valve is not functioning correctly.
fullDescription: The E19 error code indicates the diverter valve, which directs water between the upper and lower spray arms, has malfunctioned. The valve may be stuck, the motor has failed, or the control board cannot actuate it properly.

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, then restart and run a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check for blockages
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly. Look for debris in the sump area that could block the diverter valve.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the diverter valve in the sump area. Check if it moves freely. Clean any debris.

fixSteps.title: Replace the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve motor has failed, replace the diverter assembly. Disconnect wiring and mounting hardware and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the diverter motor has failed and the sump needs disassembly to access it.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E19 diverter valve fix

faq.q: What does the diverter valve do?
faq.a: It alternates water flow between the upper and lower spray arms to ensure all racks receive adequate cleaning.

faq.q: Can I still use the dishwasher with E19?
faq.a: The dishwasher may run but cleaning will be uneven, with either the top or bottom rack not getting proper water flow.

faq.q: How do I know if the top or bottom is affected?
faq.a: If dishes on one level are consistently dirty while the other is clean, the diverter valve may be stuck in one position.

title: Wash Pump Motor Error
shortDescription: The wash pump motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The E21 error code indicates the main circulation/wash pump motor has encountered a problem. The motor may be jammed, seized, or has an electrical fault. Without the wash pump, water cannot be circulated through the spray arms.

fixSteps.title: Clean filters and check for debris
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm, filter cover, and filter assembly. Look for broken glass, food debris, or foreign objects in the sump.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With filters removed, look into the pump opening and check if the impeller can rotate. Remove any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Test the pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and access the wash pump. Check the wiring connections and test the motor with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wash pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor has failed, replace the entire pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the wash pump motor needs replacement, as it requires accessing the underside of the dishwasher.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E21 wash pump error fix

faq.q: Can broken glass jam the pump?
faq.a: Yes, broken glass is a common cause. Remove all debris carefully to avoid cuts.

faq.q: Is the wash pump the same as the drain pump?
faq.a: No, the wash pump circulates water through spray arms. The drain pump removes water from the tub. They are separate components.

faq.q: How do I access the wash pump?
faq.a: The wash pump is located in the sump at the bottom of the tub. On most models it is accessible by removing the filter assembly from inside.

title: Filter Blockage / Drain Issue
shortDescription: The dishwasher filter system is clogged, causing drainage issues.
fullDescription: The E22 error code indicates the filter system is severely clogged, preventing proper water circulation and drainage. This is different from a drain pump error -- it specifically indicates the filters need cleaning. Severely clogged filters can cause poor cleaning, incomplete draining, and recirculation of dirty water.

fixSteps.title: Remove and clean all filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm, then twist and remove the cylindrical fine filter and the flat coarse filter. Clean all parts under running water.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump area
fixSteps.instruction: With the filters removed, clean the sump area of any food particles, grease, or debris using a sponge.

fixSteps.title: Check filter mesh integrity
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the fine filter mesh for tears or holes. A damaged mesh allows food particles to recirculate. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Run a hot cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Reinstall all filters. Run a hot cycle (70C) with a dishwasher cleaner to remove grease and residue from the internal components.

whenToCallPro: Rarely needed -- this is almost always resolved by thorough cleaning of the filter system.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E22 filter blockage fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the filters?
faq.a: Clean the filters at least every 1-2 weeks, or after every 5-6 wash cycles for optimal performance.

faq.q: Do I need to pre-rinse dishes?
faq.a: You do not need to pre-rinse, but scrape off large food particles before loading to prevent filter clogging.

faq.q: Can a torn filter cause problems?
faq.a: Yes, a damaged fine filter allows food particles to recirculate and can clog spray arm nozzles.

title: Drain Error / Water Not Draining
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot drain water properly.
fullDescription: The E24 error code indicates the dishwasher is unable to drain water. The drain hose may be kinked or blocked, the drain pump filter may be clogged, or the drain pump has failed. This is one of the most common Siemens dishwasher errors and is usually resolved by cleaning the drain system.
symptoms: Drain pump not running or running weakly

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose under the sink for kinks or crushes. Ensure it has a high loop or air gap. If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal to clear it.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and filter assembly. Clean the sump and check for debris blocking the drain pump.

fixSteps.title: Check disposal knockout
fixSteps.instruction: If newly installed with a garbage disposal, verify the knockout plug inside the disposal has been removed.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the drain hose and filters are clear, the drain pump motor may have failed. Listen for the pump during a drain cycle. If silent, it may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E24 drain error fix

faq.q: Why does E24 keep coming back?
faq.a: If E24 recurs, there may be a chronic issue like a partially blocked vent, a too-long drain hose, or a deteriorating pump.

faq.q: What is a high loop?
faq.a: A high loop is when the drain hose is raised to the height of the countertop before descending to the drain, preventing backflow.

faq.q: Can I drain the dishwasher manually?
faq.a: Yes, you can bail out standing water with a cup and sponge. Some Siemens models also have an emergency drain option.

title: Drain Pump Blockage
shortDescription: The drain pump impeller is blocked by debris.
fullDescription: The E25 error code specifically indicates the drain pump impeller is blocked and cannot rotate. This is a more specific version of the E24 drain error, pointing directly to a physical blockage in the pump rather than the drain line.
symptoms: Drain pump hums but water does not drain
Clicking or grinding from pump area

fixSteps.title: Access the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm and the entire filter assembly from the bottom of the tub to expose the drain pump cover.

fixSteps.title: Remove pump cover
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the pump cover (twist or unscrew depending on model). Check for and remove any foreign objects, glass, or debris blocking the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Check impeller rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the pump impeller spins freely by rotating it with your finger. It should spin smoothly without resistance.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the impeller is damaged or the motor does not run after clearing debris, the pump assembly needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the pump motor itself has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E25 drain pump blockage fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E24 and E25?
faq.a: E24 is a general drain failure. E25 specifically indicates the drain pump impeller is physically blocked.

faq.q: Can broken glass damage the pump?
faq.a: Yes, glass fragments can jam or damage the impeller. Be careful when removing glass from the pump area.

faq.q: How do I prevent pump blockages?
faq.a: Always scrape large food particles off dishes before loading, and check the filters regularly.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The incoming power supply voltage is outside the acceptable range.
fullDescription: The E27 error code indicates the dishwasher control board has detected that the incoming power supply voltage is too low. This typically occurs during periods of peak electrical demand or when the electrical circuit is shared with other high-draw appliances.

causes.cause: Low mains voltage during peak demand

symptoms: Other appliances on circuit also affected

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: If E27 appeared during a period of heavy electrical use in your home, wait until other appliances are off and try again.

fixSteps.title: Check the circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the dishwasher is on a dedicated circuit not shared with other high-draw appliances like microwaves or toasters.

fixSteps.title: Test outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to check the voltage at the dishwasher's electrical connection. It should be within 10% of the rated voltage (typically 220-240V in Europe, 120V in North America).

fixSteps.title: Call an electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If the voltage is consistently low, an electrician should inspect the circuit, junction box, and panel for loose connections or undersized wiring.

whenToCallPro: If the voltage at the outlet is consistently below the acceptable range, a licensed electrician should inspect the circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E27 low voltage error fix

faq.q: Is E27 a dishwasher fault?
faq.a: Usually not. E27 typically indicates a problem with the electrical supply, not the dishwasher itself.

faq.q: Can a voltage stabilizer help?
faq.a: A voltage stabilizer or UPS can help if your mains voltage is consistently unstable, but fixing the underlying electrical issue is preferred.

faq.q: Should the dishwasher be on a dedicated circuit?
faq.a: Yes, the dishwasher should ideally be on its own dedicated electrical circuit for proper operation.

title: Turbidity/Aqua Sensor Error
shortDescription: The water clarity sensor (Aqua Sensor) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E28 error code indicates the Aqua Sensor (turbidity sensor) is not functioning correctly. This sensor measures how cloudy or dirty the wash water is, allowing automatic programs to adjust water usage and cycle duration. A faulty sensor leads to inefficient washing.
symptoms: Dishes not clean on auto settings

fixSteps.title: Clean the Aqua Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the Aqua Sensor in the sump area. Clean the sensor lens with a soft cloth and warm water. Remove any grease or scale.

fixSteps.title: Descale the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If scale buildup is present, use white vinegar on a cloth to dissolve mineral deposits from the sensor window.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and check the sensor wiring connections. Test the sensor with a multimeter if accessible.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, the sensor may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If sensor replacement is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E28 aqua sensor fix

faq.q: What is the Aqua Sensor?
faq.a: The Aqua Sensor measures water clarity (turbidity) to optimize wash programs automatically, saving water and energy.

faq.q: Will E28 affect cleaning?
faq.a: Auto programs will not adjust properly without the sensor, potentially using too much or too little water.

faq.q: Can I use non-auto programs with E28?
faq.a: Yes, manually selected programs with fixed water levels should work normally even with a faulty Aqua Sensor.

title: Main Control Board / Software Error
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an internal software or hardware error.
fullDescription: The E31 error code indicates the main control board has experienced a software crash or hardware fault. The processor may have locked up, or an internal communication error has occurred. A power reset may clear a temporary glitch, but persistent E31 typically requires board replacement.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 10 minutes to fully discharge the board. Turn back on and test.

fixSteps.title: Check board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Open the door and remove the inner panel. Check all wire harness connections on the control board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for visible damage on the control board such as burn marks, corrosion, or swollen capacitors.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after reset and connections are checked, the control board needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power resets, professional diagnosis is needed to confirm the board is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E31 control board error fix

faq.q: Is E31 always a board failure?
faq.a: No, a single occurrence often clears with a power reset. Recurring E31 indicates a failing control board.

faq.q: Can I reprogram the control board?
faq.a: No, consumer-level reprogramming is not available. The board must be replaced if it has a firmware issue.

faq.q: Should I use a surge protector?
faq.a: Yes, a surge protector can help protect the electronic control board from power surges that can cause E31.

title: Water Level Sensor / Pressure Switch Error
shortDescription: The water level sensor is not providing correct readings.
fullDescription: The E05 error code indicates the water level pressure switch or its associated components are malfunctioning. The dishwasher cannot accurately determine the water level in the tub, which may lead to overfilling, underfilling, or failure to start the wash cycle.
symptoms: Cycle does not progress past fill

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then turn it back on and test.

fixSteps.title: Check the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the pressure switch (usually at the side of the tub). Check the small rubber hose for kinks, cracks, or blockages.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the hose from the switch and gently blow into the switch port. You should hear a click as the switch activates.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixSteps.instruction: If the switch does not click or provides incorrect readings, replace it. Remove the mounting clip, disconnect hose and wiring, and install a new switch.

whenToCallPro: If the pressure switch and hose are fine but the error persists, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E05 pressure switch error fix

faq.q: What does the pressure switch do?
faq.a: It measures the water level inside the dishwasher by detecting air pressure changes in a small hose connected to the tub.

faq.q: Can a blocked hose cause overfilling?
faq.a: Yes, if the hose is blocked, the switch cannot detect rising water levels and the dishwasher may overfill.

faq.q: Is E05 common?
faq.a: E05 is less common than drain or fill errors, but the pressure hose can become clogged with debris over time.

title: Drying Fan Error
shortDescription: The drying fan is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: The E07 error code indicates the drying fan motor is not running or is not reaching the expected speed. On models equipped with active drying using a fan, this error prevents the drying phase from operating. The dishes will still be washed but will not be dried by forced air.

causes.cause: Control board not powering the fan

symptoms: No fan noise during dry phase

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restart and run a full cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check the fan and air duct
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Locate the drying fan (usually on the inner door panel or side of the tub). Check for blockages in the air duct and ensure the fan blade spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring connections to the fan motor. Use a multimeter to test the motor for continuity.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the fan motor has failed, disconnect the wiring, remove the mounting screws, and install a new motor.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor appears fine but does not receive power from the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens dishwasher E07 drying fan error fix

faq.q: Does my Siemens dishwasher have a drying fan?
faq.a: Not all models have active drying fans. Higher-end models like the iQ500 and iQ700 series typically include this feature.

faq.q: Can I still wash dishes with E07?
faq.a: Yes, the wash function is not affected. Only the active drying will not work. Dishes can be air-dried with the door slightly open.

faq.q: Is E07 the same as E10?
faq.a: Both relate to drying issues. E07 specifically targets the fan motor, while E10 is a more general drying system error.
